title: Fig. 3 in Cloning, expression and characterisation of a cysteine protease from Trichinella spiralis
author: Wang, Yan Yan Song Li Ang Wang Hua Na Ren Xin Qi Ge Ge Sun Ruo Dan Liu Peng Jiang Xi Zhang Jing Cui Zhong Quan
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Fig. 3. Quantitative real-time PCR (qPCR) analysis of transcription levels of the cysteine proteinase of Trichinella spiralis (TsCP) in various life cycle stages of Trichinella spiralis (Owen, 1835). The transcription level of TsCP in AW at 3 and 6 days post-infection, muscle larvae (ML), intestinal infective larvae (IIL) and newborn larvae were analysed using the Ct (2-ΔΔCT) ratio, with glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase as an internal control. The transcription level of TsCP in AW stage was significantly higher than those in all of the larval stages. Asterisk indicates significant differences (P <0.05); hashtag P> 0.05 compared between the ML and IIL stages.
